title: tpm — project_tracking_stakeholder_status_email — v1
description: Synthesized by skill_builder for persona=tpm, kb=project_tracking_stakeholder_status_email. Refine before promoting.

project_name.description: Extract the project/program name as stated on the tracking page header or in the Project Overview/Scope section; if multiple names appear, choose the most repeated/primary name used in links and headings.

project_overview.description: Extract the 1–2 sentence Project Overview from the tracking page's 'Project Overview' section; keep it exec-safe and avoid internal acronyms unless expanded in the source.

executive_summary_status_raw.description: Extract the 'Executive Summary Status' block verbatim (light cleanup allowed for readability) from the tracking page; preserve key links/ticket references included in that block.

rag_summary.description: Derive a single RAG (Red/Amber/Green) status with a 1-sentence justification by aggregating: (a) WBS task statuses (e.g., GreenComplete vs in-progress), (b) dated WBS status updates from the last ~7 days, and (c) open bug tracking items; format as 'RAG: <color> — <justification>'.

schedule_health.description: Derive schedule health (On track / At risk / Off track) with a 1-sentence justification by combining: WBS due dates (if present), dependency notes, and the most recent dated status updates indicating slippage or completion; if due dates are missing, state that schedule confidence is limited due to missing dates and base the assessment on progress cadence.

blocking_issues.description: Derive the list of open blockers by aggregating: (1) items explicitly labeled as issues/bugs in 'Bug Tracking', and (2) WBS status updates that indicate 'blocked', 'waiting on', 'dependency', or 'issue encountered'; for each blocker include description, owner (team/person if stated), target resolution date if stated, and whether it appears critical-path based on dependency language.

next_steps.description: Derive time-bound next steps by summarising action-oriented statements from the most recent WBS status updates and any explicit action items on the page; each item must include an owner (team/person) and a due timeframe ('by <date>' if present, otherwise 'this sprint' or 'next week' based on the update context).

exec_asks.description: Derive executive asks by aggregating implicit requests/needs from WBS updates, dependency notes, and bug tracking commentary (e.g., requests for environments, approvals, resourcing); only include asks that are explicitly implied by the source text, and for each include the ask, the requester/owner, and the decision/assistance needed.

workstreams.description: Extract the list of workstreams/teams from the 'Project Areas/Leads' and stakeholder team sections (e.g., SCM, FAaaS team, HCP team, ADP); include the lead/POC if stated next to the area.

stakeholders_people.description: Extract named stakeholders and their roles from the stakeholder roster section (e.g., Business Sponsor); keep names and roles only.

stakeholder_emails.description: Extract stakeholder email addresses listed on the tracking page; do not infer missing emails.

project_jira_links.description: Extract the Jira links listed under 'Project Jira Links' (or equivalent); keep as a list of URLs or key+URL if present.

project_repository_link.description: Extract the project repository or project folder link from the 'Project Repository' section.

slack_channel_labels.description: Extract Slack channel references and any project labels from the 'Slack Channel/Labels' section.

test_plan_link.description: Extract the Test Plan link if present on the tracking page.

feature_flags.description: Extract feature flag enablement entries (flag name and value/state) from the feature flag section; keep concise.

recent_updates_by_date.description: Derive a weekly delta list by aggregating dated entries from WBS 'Task Status Updates' within the last ~7 days relative to the most recent date on the page; include date and 1-line summary of what changed.

agenda_email_eml_draft.description: Generate a reviewable RFC-822 .eml draft (plain text body is acceptable) for a weekly tracking meeting agenda email using extracted fields: include subject, To/Cc placeholders, and a structured agenda with sections for orm_status (if unavailable, explicitly mark as 'Not provided on source page'), rag_summary, schedule_health, blocking_issues, and per-workstream next_steps and exec_asks; do not send email.
